questions.q: In what year was the Second Battle of Panipat fought?
questions.explanation: The Second Battle of Panipat was fought in 1556, early in Akbar's reign.

questions.q: The Second Battle of Panipat helped secure the throne of which young Mughal emperor?
questions.explanation: The Mughal victory restored the dynasty's hold on northern India for the young emperor Akbar.

questions.q: Who commanded the Mughal army on behalf of the young Akbar at the Second Battle of Panipat?
questions.explanation: Bairam Khan, Akbar's regent and guardian, led the Mughal forces at Panipat in 1556.

questions.q: Who was the general that opposed the Mughals at the Second Battle of Panipat?
questions.explanation: Hemu, a Hindu general who had seized Delhi, faced the Mughal army at Panipat.

questions.q: On which town's battlefield in present-day Haryana was this battle fought?
questions.explanation: The battle was fought on the historic field of Panipat in present-day Haryana.

questions.q: Which side won the Second Battle of Panipat?
questions.explanation: The Mughals won, restoring their dynasty's control over northern India.

questions.q: Just before this battle, Hemu had captured which major city?
questions.explanation: Hemu had seized Delhi (and Agra) before the Mughals marched against him at Panipat.

questions.q: The Second Battle of Panipat took place during the early years of which dynasty's rule?
questions.explanation: The battle came early in the Mughal dynasty's reign, just after Humayun's restoration and death.

questions.q: The Second Battle of Panipat occurred just after the death of which Mughal emperor?
questions.explanation: Humayun died in 1556, and the battle was fought soon after to secure his son Akbar's throne.

questions.q: How many battles in total have famously been fought at Panipat in Indian history?
questions.explanation: Three major battles were fought at Panipat: in 1526, 1556, and 1761.

questions.q: What title had Hemu assumed after his early victories and capture of Delhi?
questions.explanation: Hemu took the ancient royal title Vikramaditya after seizing Delhi and Agra.

questions.q: Hemu had served as a general and minister under which preceding dynasty?
questions.explanation: Hemu rose as a commander and chief minister under the Afghan Sur dynasty rulers.

questions.q: A key feature of Hemu's army at Panipat was a large force of which animals?
questions.explanation: Hemu fielded a powerful corps of war elephants, which initially gave his army an advantage.

questions.q: What event during the fighting turned the tide decisively in the Mughals' favour?
options: Hemu was struck in the eye by an arrow
questions.explanation: Hemu was wounded in the eye by an arrow and fell unconscious, throwing his army into confusion.

questions.q: What happened to Hemu after the battle?
options: He was pardoned and given a post
He died of natural causes years later
questions.explanation: The wounded and captured Hemu was brought before the Mughal camp and executed.

questions.q: Which emperor's victory at the First Battle of Panipat (1526) had earlier founded the Mughal Empire?
questions.explanation: Babur defeated Ibrahim Lodi at the First Battle of Panipat in 1526 to found the Mughal Empire.

questions.q: Roughly how old was Akbar at the time of the Second Battle of Panipat?
questions.explanation: Akbar was only about thirteen, so the campaign was directed by his regent Bairam Khan.

questions.q: From which low-status background had Hemu risen, making his career notable?
questions.explanation: Hemu rose from humble origins as a trader before becoming a formidable general and administrator.

questions.q: The Mughal advance to Panipat in 1556 was partly to recover which capital that Hemu had taken?
questions.explanation: The Mughals marched to retake Delhi, which Hemu had captured shortly before the battle.

questions.q: The Second Battle of Panipat is best described as a struggle to decide control of which region?
questions.explanation: Victory at Panipat confirmed Mughal control over northern India in Akbar's early reign.

questions.q: Some advisers urged Bairam Khan to avoid battle, but he chose to do what?
questions.explanation: Despite cautious counsel, Bairam Khan resolved to confront and defeat Hemu at Panipat.

questions.q: What was the principal historical significance of the Mughal win at Panipat in 1556?
options: It re-established Mughal rule in north India
questions.explanation: The victory secured the restoration of Mughal authority in northern India under Akbar.

questions.q: Hemu is sometimes called the last Hindu ruler to sit on the throne of Delhi before which long period?
questions.explanation: Hemu's brief reign at Delhi as Vikramaditya is often noted as the last Hindu ascendancy there before sustained Mughal rule.

questions.q: Under which Sur ruler had Hemu most recently served before making his bid for power?
questions.explanation: Hemu served the Sur ruler Adil Shah Suri as his chief minister and general before seizing Delhi himself.

questions.q: Hemu reportedly remained undefeated in many engagements before Panipat. About how many battles is he traditionally said to have won?
questions.explanation: Tradition credits Hemu with winning around twenty-two battles before his final defeat at Panipat.

questions.q: What was the name of Hemu's famous war elephant said to be present at the battle?
questions.explanation: Hemu's renowned war elephant was named Hawai; Chetak was instead Maharana Pratap's horse.

questions.q: After Hemu fell wounded, who is traditionally said to have struck the final blow that killed the captured commander?
options: Bairam Khan (with the young Akbar present)
questions.explanation: Accounts state that the captured, unconscious Hemu was executed by Bairam Khan, with the young Akbar present in camp.

questions.q: The Mughal artillery park at the battle was largely directed by which officer under Bairam Khan?
questions.explanation: Ali Quli Khan, later titled Khan-i-Zaman, was among the leading Mughal commanders of the vanguard and guns at Panipat.

questions.q: Hemu had earlier in 1556 defeated Mughal forces and captured Delhi at which battle?
questions.explanation: Hemu defeated the Mughal governor near Delhi (at Tughlaqabad) in 1556 before the showdown at Panipat.

questions.q: The Second Battle of Panipat is sometimes seen as ending whose dream of restoring Hindu sovereignty over Delhi?
questions.explanation: Hemu's defeat ended his short-lived attempt to rule from Delhi as Vikramaditya.
